With major health concerns, time is not on Michael's side, so the clan embarks on a trial week sampling the life they could have 'Down Under'. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Ashford, Kent, a report of a burglary ends with a police officer fighting a man armed with a knife. Plus, other officers in the county get to grips with taser training

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Eric Knowles and experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique-hunting in Wrexham, north Wales, before heading to Stockport for the auction. Eric is also faced with a mystery where the answer might be under his nose

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King visit beaches and mountains in Thailand and discover how the country's multicultural history and its place at the heart of the spice trade have helped create a rich and varied cuisine. They taste a traditional Thai breakfast and cook their own jungle curry for a remote hill tribe

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max head out into wine country to investigate the killing of a dog at one of the island's most famous vineyards - Bodegas Negra. It transpires that the shooting is just one of an increasingly dramatic series of threats faced by property owner Hans Webber, his daughter Yvonne and son-in-law Rainer. Later, when Rainer's latest batch of white wine is dyed blue, all fingers point to arch-rival Emilio Byass

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to Dorset to help relocate a retired doctor and her husband who are selling up their Surrey family home. They have an £800,000 budget to spend on a spacious country property where they can enjoy their respective hobbies. Jonnie also heads out into the waters of West Bay to see how a conservation scheme is helping protect healthy levels of marine life and the livelihoods of local fishermen

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage stereoscope, two gigantic apothecary glasses and a children's game thought to be from the 19th century

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the week with a lively mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson-Bennett, former England fast-bowler Steve Harmison, world champion swimmer Karen Pickering and ex-Everton and Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy takes action against Sharon, sending in the removal men and pleading with her to reveal what has happened to her son. Rainie confronts Max about his problems and tells him he is not fit to look after Abi. Mick goes to the police station to review his statement about Katy, while the truth comes out for Chelsea and Lucas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil looks out for Sharon on a tough day that sees her both celebrating Albie's first birthday and mourning Dennis's death. Kush and Whitney have no plans for Valentine's Day, so they watch a movie together at home - but when there is a power cut, they find themselves getting much closer. Stuart convinces Keegan to buy Tiffany a bag - but Rainie points out it is not the expensive handbag his wife wanted

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A stag do ends in tragedy when the host of a celebratory fishing trip is found harpooned on a beach the following morning. As the boat never moved, DI Neville Parker and his team struggle to determine how anyone in attendance could have been responsible. Caribbean-set detective drama, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Oscar-nominated Get Out star Daniel Kaluuya, who is appearing in crime biopic Judas and the Black Messiah. Hollywood star Sienna Miller talks about her surreal drama Wander Darkly, while Mayim Bialik chats about starring in new Miranda-based sitcom Call Me Kat. Hustle star Adrian Lester promotes the live-streaming new play Hymn, and comedians - and husband and wife - Marcus Brigstocke and Rachel Parris are appear. Plus, British singer-songwriter Arlo Parks performs her current single Caroline

programme.title: The Notebook (2004)
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about a passionate young couple whose fledgling affair is smothered almost before it has begun. Romantic drama, starring Ryan Gosling, Rachel McAdams, Gena Rowlands and James Garner

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ken Hom and Ching-He Huang and special guest Mica Paris. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the post-war British modern art movement in the seaside town of St Ives, the home for leading artists such as Barbara Hepworth and Terry Frost. At the Lizard Peninsula, Rick heads out to sea to fish for red mullet, and on the wild coast at Land's End, he discovers why Cornwall has a rather unusual animal as its national emblem

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day six of the first Grand Slam event of the year, featuring third-round singles matches at Melbourne Park

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is house hunting in Somerset with a couple who have a £675,000 budget to find a rural home that must have annexe potential for their parents

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: A group of dealers bid against each other for items collected by the public including two teddies, an Islamic tile, a painting and a chair brought in by a 12-year-old boy

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Wales (Kick-off 4.45pm). All the action from the match in the second round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Scotland won in Wales for the first time in 18 years last season, prevailing 14-10 with the help of replacement hooker Stuart McInally's second-half try, and they will go into this match with all the more confidence after winning their opener away to England. However, the Welsh will be hoping to improve considerably this time around after a disappointing 2020 campaign that followed a Grand Slam the previous year, and got off to a good start with a victory at home to Ireland. Gabby Logan presents, with analysis from Jonathan Davies, Jeremy Guscott and John Barclay, commentary by Andrew Cotter, Sam Warburton and Chris Paterson, and pitchside reports from Andy Nicol

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a writers edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Jane Fallon, Fiona Neill, Nadia Shireen, Conn Iggulden, Richard Madeley, Allison Pearson, Tim Weaver and Jill Mansell

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: When Fenisha's life is in danger, Ethan must race against time to try and save his family. Meanwhile, Connie is determined to finally get things right with her daughter Grace, and Faith realises she has to put her family first, and decides to break off her arrangement with Dylan. Starring Olivia D'Lima, George Rainsford and Kirsty Mitchell

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Following the tragedy at the hospital, Fleming continues to work under cover in missing persons and looks into the disappearance of 15-year-old Carly Kirk, a case in which Denton appears to have a personal interest. The under-suspicion detective also starts to scrutinise the background of the AC-12 officers and gathers compromising evidence to use against the anti-corruption team

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur. The Reds recorded a comfortable 3-0 win when they met Leicester at Anfield in November, but the Foxes have enjoyed a strong campaign so far on the whole. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking demonstrate how much they had improved since a 2-0 defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium three months ago

programme.title: Sliding Doors (1998)
programme.desc: A London woman's entire future hinges on whether or not she catches a train. In one version of events, she gets the chance to start afresh and lead a happy new life, but in the other she is stuck supporting her lazy, unfaithful boyfriend. Comedy drama, presenting two parallel stories featuring the same character. Starring Gwyneth Paltrow, John Hannah and John Lynch

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur. The Reds recorded a comfortable 3-0 win when they met Leicester at Anfield in November, but the Foxes have enjoyed a strong campaign so far on the whole. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking demonstrate how much they had improved since a 2-0 defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium three months ago

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts moral and religious discussions on whether Britain is still racist, and would the world be a better place if everyone believed in reincarnation

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Ferris-Garbers yearn for the countryside on their doorstep and are hoping that a week in mum Caitlin's native Norfolk will convince their reluctant sons to leave London behind

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin oversees proceedings as Tim Weeks and Richard Madley helps two teams look for items at the South of England Showground in Ardingly, West Sussex

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits Ripon Cathedral to hear reflections on Lent, and Katie Piper meets a Christian in Leeds who donated a kidney to a stranger

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall where she sifts through the junk in search of two items that can be transformed. Upholsterer Ray Clarke transforms a traditional wing-back chair and there's a surprise in store with a rug Sarah found that could mean some cash to return to its unsuspecting owner

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in Suffolk with a north London couple who have a budget of £640,000, and also meets a goat farmer who made his own escape from the city to the Stour valley

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the recent meetings in Lievin and New York. With commentary by Steve Cram

programme.title: Monsters University (2013)
programme.desc: Student creatures Mike Wazowski and James P Sullivan, aka Sulley, attend university to learn how to be scary, but their rivalry gets them both kicked off the course. The monstrous duo must learn how to work together if they are to realise their dreams of terrifying children. Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is at Kentish Town City farm to lend a helping hand as well as taking a look back through the programme's archives to celebrate winter heroes, from the farmers toughing it out in all weathers to members of a community working together to save the heart of their village. Margherita also catches up with one of her childhood heroes in former Olympic ski jumper Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Bodnant Garden, a National Trust property near Tal-y-Cafn in Conwy, where items assessed include an ecclesiastical candlestick from a very special cathedral and some novelty glass bottles with a hefty price tag. There is also a tandem bicycle and sidecar and a selection of 20th-century automata

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: The net begins to close on Charles and Marie-Andree in India,and he hatches a daring plan to escape to South America, but he is becoming reckless and soon everything spirals out of control. In Bangkok, Herman, Angela and the Action Committee face up to life after the Sobhraj case

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Arsenal v Leeds United at Emirates Stadium, West Bromwich Albion v Manchester United at The Hawthorns, Southampton v Wolverhampton Wanderers at St Mary's Stadium and Everton v Fulham at Goodison Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: A Royal Team Talk: Tackling Mental Health
programme.desc: The Duke of Cambridge, Gareth Southgate, Peter Crouch, Thierry Henry, Danny Rose and Jermaine Jenas gather together in the company of Dan Walker for an extraordinary and revealing conversation about men's mental health

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and expert auctioneer Simon Bower welcome five more people to the Bidding Room with another batch of weird and wonderful lots. The dealers bid against each other on two very decorative perfume bottles, an unusual box, two show-stopping pieces of African art, some rare ceramics and a unique light fitting

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Two retired police officers with £600,000 explore the breathtaking landscapes of north Wales on a house-hunting expedition with presenter Jules Hudson. The couple want a traditional Welsh home with at least an acre of land. Jules also tests his nerve and takes the plunge when he experiences one of the region's many extreme watersports

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team of experts bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Guitar expert Julyan Wallis is presented with a cherished 1960s jazz guitar which has been left in a broken state for decades. Meanwhile, wood restoration expert Will Kirk has his work cut out when is presented with a handmade veneered card table. Later, the experts turn their attention to bookmakers bag and a Victorian clockwork pigeon

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright revisits his favourite exercises from the week

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: The playwright's use of relationships and historical figures in his works

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: This edition shows how the monasteries played a key role in providing welfare and charity for those in need. The team helps to restore a corrody room, which would have been granted to an elderly worker as a form of pension. Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold gather and roast limestone for the floor and Ruth Goodman harvests rushes from the river to make a mattress. They also help the abbot prepare for a big feast to entertain a patron, with Ruth tackling the laundry and Tom overseeing the production of a book to give as a gift

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Ruth Goodman, Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold bring in the barley and celebrate with a harvest feast, while Ruth prepares for winter by salting meat to preserve it. As the team plans to put on a mystery play, Tom creates fireworks with an alchemist, while Ruth and Peter help with props and costumes. Preparing to leave the farm, they reflect on what they have learned and contemplate what the dissolution of the monasteries meant for the kingdom

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk celebrates food created for royal jubilees, with chef Anna Haugh using unusual 19th-century ingredients found in a dish created for Queen Victoria's golden one

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Hannibal's son Wilson leaves the Commandos to go in search of a female to start a family of his own, but his loyalty is later tested when the Whiskers attack his old clan. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: From Powderham Castle near Exeter, where Will Axon gets to grips with a mysterious wooden club and Claire Rawle is taken by a dove brooch. Presenter Paul Martin explores a lighthouse that moved from perilous rocks to Plymouth Hoe

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kae Kurd, Zoe Lyons, Andrew Hunter Murray and Kate Robbins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games for the final time, prior to the week's overall winner being declared

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions in a bid to win a jackpot of £3,000

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef concludes his tour with trips to the Languedoc and Provence. In the border town of Uzes, Rick samples salt cod and marvels at a colourful array of French patisserie - selecting a seasonal fig tart to have with his morning coffee. He moves on to the harbour town of Cassis to try North African lamb chorba cooked by a local fisherman, while back in his country kitchen, he prepares a confit tomato and aubergine tarte tatin

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The horticulturalist explores gardens of early independent America, learning how the history of the South is still very much evident in the places and people he meets. He visits the famous garden where President Thomas Jefferson, one of America's founding fathers, enjoyed spending his time seeking out seeds and plants for his 1,000-ft vegetable garden. From there he heads to Charleston, which boasts some of the most beautiful trees in the world and learns about the life of the plantations there. Later, in Miami, Monty visits one of the most spectacular Renaissance recreations built on marshland

programme.title: Stonehenge: The Lost Circle Revealed
programme.desc: Using cutting-edge research, a dedicated team of archaeologists has compiled the evidence to fill in a 400-year gap in man's knowledge of the bluestones of Stonehenge. By combining innovative 3D scanning techniques, traditional field archaeology and novel laboratory analysis, the team has discovered where the stones were quarried and where they first stood. Professor Alice Roberts shows how the team made its discoveries and joins Professor Mike Parker Pearson to piece together the final parts of the puzzle, solving one of the toughest challenges ever faced by archaeologists

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on the theme of roaming this week, with Sara Pascoe, Josh Widdicombe and Benjamin Zephaniah and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark introduces analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Lady Macbeth (2016)
programme.desc: A young woman newly married to a rich industrialist in 19th-century England finds herself starved of companionship - so when her husband is called away to one of his collieries, she begins a passionate and fiery affair with a young groom from the estate. Drama, starring Florence Pugh, Cosmo Jarvis and Paul Hilton

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: D'Anna Biers is willing to take extreme measures to find out if Baltar is hiding information vital to the survival of the Cylons. Meanwhile, Admiral Adama and Roslin decide whether to put Apollo's plan into action and launch a biological weapon against their enemy. Sci-fi drama, starring James Callis and Edward James Olmos

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Three Cylon raiders are expected to mount an attack, but a desperate plea from one forces Starbuck to hold fire. Adama orders her to escort the pilot after learning his identity - Danny 'Bulldog' Novacek - a man taken prisoner three years ago while he and the Admiral were on a mission. Guilt-ridden Adama then struggles with the prospect of handing over the captive and aiding the conquest of the 12 colonies

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: A trio of siblings plan the perfect canine-themed wedding, complete with a dog agility course for their parents and a guest appearance by CBBC's Dodge

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The presenters look back at some amazing dens from the first two series. Lauren Layfield revisits the train, magical library, community and plane den

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Chef Omari makes his twist on the Jamaican classic stew peas, packed with healthy vegetables, spice and special dumplings

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall sets out to visit six continents in search of Earth's 60 deadliest animals

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers defeat rival gang the Zappas in a fight over territory, while Flower gives birth to another litter. However, tragedy strikes, and a single act threatens to change the face of the manor for ever

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers attempt to come to terms with the loss of an important member, and Flower's daughter Rocket Dog takes charge of the gang, but struggles to settle into her new role

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Zaphod leaves the Whiskers due to the imminent change in season. However, as he sets out to make his own way in the world, he is confronted by an old adversary

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Just as the annual lambing season gets into full swing, coronavirus grips the nation, and the farmers must work hard to keep their businesses afloat. In Aberdeenshire, new entrant farmers Isla and George lose their childcare, meaning Isla is left to look after the livestock and farm shop with three young children in tow. Elsewhere in the Lake District, Matt and Dani are faced with challenges when the usual team of students they rely on during their busiest time of year can no longer help with the calving

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don journeys to the Mediterranean, the cradle of European civilisation, for the next stage in his horticultural tour. In Italy, he witnesses the elaborate stone and water gardens created during the high Renaissance. He also investigates the influence of Moorish culture on European gardens, visiting the Aguedal in Marrakesh, and Spain's magnificent Alhambra

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The competition reaches the halfway point, where the contestants are challenged by Mary Berry to prepare the ultimate Sunday roast. The only rule is that the meal must involve roasted meat and some form of potatoes - they are otherwise free to do whatever they want. Two cooks compete to produce the best pork crackling, while another adds a Morrocan twist. For the second task, Chris Bavin challenges the cooks to make banana bread without using flour, while the final round involves a classic French dessert

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Neil and Clare have a garden with a stunning view of rural Cheshire, but they cannot decide how to turn the space into somewhere to benefit all of their large family. Two designers each come up with a new outdoor plan, pitching it to the couple through the use of virtual reality technology. But which one will they choose? Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: An exploration of the romantic comedy, one of the most successful movie genres of all time, featuring interviews with on-screen couples including Doris Day and Rock Hudson, and Meg Ryan and Tom Hanks. The documentary also highlights how the rom-com has, over the years, given many female actors the opportunity to shine over their male co-stars, and looks at the 'Hugh factor' - and the impact of Richard Curtis's Four Weddings and a Funeral on Hollywood romance in the 21st century

programme.title: The Wedding Planner (2001)
programme.desc: San Francisco wedding planner Mary Fiore organises other people's nuptials but has never had much luck in love. One day she is swept off her feet by handsome doctor Steve Edison. They spend a magical night together and all seems perfect, but her dreams of him becoming her husband suffer a setback when she discovers she has already been hired to organise his marriage to another woman. Will true love find a way? Director Adam Shankman's romantic comedy, starring Jennifer Lopez, Matthew McConaughey, Bridgette Wilson-Sampras, Justin Chambers and Joanna Gleason

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Plant and Claire Rawle find interesting items at Ashridge House in Hertfordshire, while Paul Martin visits the farm that inspired George Orwell to write Animal Farm

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Michelle Ogundehin splits the contestants into three teams, each of which is given an open-plan office space to transform for a technology client, a sustainable beauty brand and a media team. The brief is to create a work space, a break-out space and a meeting space within the office, and Michelle will be looking for both displays of signature style and work that suits the needs of the client

programme.title: Stormborn
programme.desc: The animals in countries on the northern edges of the Atlantic Ocean face worsening weather in autumn and the first stirrings of winter. Grey seals and reindeer turn their attentions to mating, hormones run wild as males compete and young animals venture away from their parents for the first time. Ewan McGregor narrates

programme.title: Perry and Croft: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The celebration of comedy writing duo David Croft and Jimmy Perry continues with an examination of how their sitcoms chronicled the UK's class system in the 20th century, looking at its depiction in Dad's Army, It Ain't Half Hot Mum, Hi-de-Hi! and You Rang, M'Lord?

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring and his platoon have their hands full with two German prisoners and the arrival of a suspicious Polish captain, while Walker sees a way to make easy money. Comedy first broadcast in 1968, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on animals beginning with the letter R, with Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: The Deer Hunter (1978)
programme.desc: The lives of three American steelworkers and their families are torn apart in the wake of the Vietnam War. After enduring horrific treatment in a prison camp during the conflict, the three friends return to their homes but struggle to readjust to normal life. Oscar-winning drama, starring Robert De Niro, Christopher Walken, Meryl Streep, John Savage and John Cazale in his last film role

programme.title: The Program (2015)
programme.desc: A journalist follows Lance Armstrong's rise to dominate the world of professional cycling, even following a successful battle with cancer. As time goes on, he begins to suspect the champion is boosting his performance with drugs, and in the face of condemnation from the media, sets out to expose the truth. Fact-based drama, starring Ben Foster, Chris O'Dowd and Dustin Hoffman

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Weather forecaster Carol Kirkwood takes a journey through the world of horticulture, receiving inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters. The series progresses in alphabetical order, and this programme features themes beginning with the letter A, and includes archive material from Gardeners' World, Countryfile and The Gardener's Year

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is on Crompton Moor in the southern Pennines to discover what makes this site of biological interest such a special place. Battered by rain, wind and snow, she learns how a plant is helping to restore these uplands, and meets a community coming together to bring wildlife back to the hills. Adam Henson tries out a new app to show how contented his animals are, wildlife film-maker Richard Taylor-Jones shows viewers how to spot wildlife on their doorsteps and Tom Heap investigates the mental health crisis in rural areas

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers have to protect their pups from a deadly enemy, while Mozart and her sister Kinkajoo are forced to depend on one another for survival. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Hannibal's son Wilson leaves the Commandos to go in search of a female to start a family of his own, but his loyalty is later tested when the Whiskers attack his old clan. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden explores the Scottish Borders by hot-air balloon, and discovers the role art and literature played in creating memorable gardens. She visits Abbotsford House, the former residence of writer Walter Scott, where she tries her hand at fly-fishing, and meets a woman whose family history at the property spans a century. She then visits Little Sparta in the Pentland Hills and examines a garden poem, before learning how the Royal Botanic Garden in Edinburgh was created

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: On a route from Pwll to Kidwelly, Derek Brockway turns Pied Piper as he launches a walk of 800 children marking the anniversary of 'People Power' that saved a stretch of the Loughor Estuary for families to enjoy. On this Carmarthenshire coastal walk, the weatherman also discovers a former munitions factory that employed 6,000 people, has a spin in a racing car and learns the story Wales' feisty warrior princess Gwenllian

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwall's most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the county's oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: In the quarter-final, the remaining celebrities show off their celebration cakes for Mary Berry's Ultimate Challenge. They also take part in Chris Bavin's all-vegetarian Rustle Up, tasked with making the humble chickpea the star of their dishes, in a bid to avoid Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates how to make ingredients go just that little bit further, adding grated beetroot to make a tasty meatball supper and using a small garden harvest to create a pudding

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day seven of the first Grand Slam event of the year, featuring fourth-round singles matches at Melbourne Park

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Death (1946)
programme.desc: A Second World War bomber pilot survives jumping out of his stricken plane without a parachute and falls in love with the radio operator he had first spoken to during the fateful flight. A heavenly messenger is sent to tell him he should have died and that his presence is required in the afterlife, but he remains determined to continue his life on Earth and insists on a trial to resolve the issue. Powell and Pressburger's romantic fantasy, starring David Niven, Kim Hunter, Roger Livesey and Marius Goring

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The celebrities continue to settle into life in their 16th-century mansion, and are brought closer together when they celebrate Amanda Barrie's 81st birthday together. Lionel Blair - who has struggled with a distended belly since having treatment for cancer - tries out a local alternative medicine in the hope of finding a solution to the problem. Bill Oddie goes in search of wildlife with a trip into the jungle, and opens up about how spending time in nature has helped him cope with mental-health issues

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The fishing grounds of St Ives Bay have supported the fleets of two ports for generations, but now change threatens the future of the fishermen of Hayle. A vast new development with hundreds of homes and leisure facilities is under construction, and the fishermen fear that their days working from their compound next to it may be numbered

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the second round of fixtures, featuring England v Italy at Twickenham, Scotland v Wales at BT Murrayfield and Ireland v France at Aviva Stadium. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Sam Warburton and Brian Moore

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Highlights of the Alpine World Ski Championships from Cortina d'Ampezzo in Italy, including the men's and women's downhill races, as the fastest skiers in the world took on icy pistes and sheer gradients in their bids for gold

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The presenter discusses the challenge of preserving unspoilt wildernesses, from the icy expanses of Siberia to the tropical forests of central and South America. Simon reflects on how his past series have dealt with the causes and impact of climate change, as well as remembering a memorable report exposing the extent of plastic pollution in the ocean. He also recalls meeting indigenous people around the world and hearing their first-hand experiences of ecological damage

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Mo Gilligan and Katherine Ryan, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan visits Bosnia and Herzegovina to discover how this small corner of Eastern Europe has changed since the war, with the aid of a local museum curator. He tries out Sarajevo's abandoned bobsleigh track, a remnant of the 1984 winter Olympics, stays in a 'war hostel', and meets a man who claims to have discovered the world's largest pyramid

programme.title: Edie (2017)
programme.desc: Seeking to fulfil a decades-old dream to climb a mountain in the Scottish Highlands, Edith Moore, an 83-year-old widow, escapes to Scotland, where she grudgingly accepts the help of Jonny, the manager of a struggling shop that sells outdoor gear. Drama, starring Sheila Hancock and Kevin Guthrie

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Nathan's ability is exposed and he loses control of the operation, forcing him to hide out in Mexico with Claire. Meanwhile, the Hunter spearheads the government's nefarious scheme and teams up with an unlikely partner to try to bring down all the heroes. Angela and Peter seek refuge in a church and begin to mend their broken relationship. Starring Adrian Pasdar, Hayden Panettiere, Cristine Rose and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: With the Hunter at the helm of the government operation, someone close to him is put in the line of fire, while Noah's marriage is pushed to breaking point. On their road trip, Hiro and Ando make a special delivery for Matt, and Angela unearths the demons of her past. Starring Zeljko Ivanek, Jack Coleman and Masi Oka

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha gets the shock of his life when a dangerous and vulnerable Jodie appears back on the scene. Kian's new boss Sahira breezes back into Holby, much to everyone's surprise. An outgoing new nurse has her first day working in a busy AAU, which proves to be a trial by fire

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on August 16, with performances by Go West, Sting, the KLF, New Kids on the Block, Together, Betty Boo, Jon Bon Jovi, Roxette, Partners in Kryme and Primal Scream

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: Celebrating some of the decade's most recognisable classics, including performances by the Carpenters, Bread, Charles Aznavour, 10cc, John Denver, the Bellamy Brothers, Exile, Captain & Tennille and Dr Hook

programme.title: Love Songs at the BBC: A Valentine's Day Special
programme.desc: A selection of romantic songs for guilty pleasures, for courtship, for declarations of love, for looking someone in the eye and whispering sweet nothings, accompanied by a compilation of some of the greatest love songs from the likes of Celine Dion, Joe Cocker and Jennifer Warnes, Jason and Kylie, 10cc and Lionel Richie, all from the Top of the Pops era

programme.title: Roy Orbison: Love Hurts
programme.desc: Documentary revealing Orbison's legacy as a beloved rock legend and a devoted father through intimate interviews with his three sons. Alex, Roy Jr and Wesley Orbison discuss the immense talent and fierce determination that provided the driving force behind their father's success and how his family life offered a strong spiritual base to escape the pressures of the rock 'n' roll lifestyle

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at The Bridge, by Marley Marl and MC Shan. The song, designed to foster community pride, ignited hip-hop's most epic rap battle as contributors including Roxanne Shante, DJ Red Alert, Funkmaster Flex and the artists themselves recall

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America
programme.desc: A look at Queen Latifah's anthem of women empowerment, 1989's Ladies First. Sexism was endemic in the hip-hop sector of the music industry during the 1980s, and Latifah recalls the lack of money put towards female rappers for marketing and music videos compared to their male counterparts

programme.title: More Dangerous Songs: And the Banned Played On
programme.desc: A compilation of songs previously banned from broadcast on the BBC, including the Kinks' Lola, Jackie by Scott Walker, and (We Don't Need This) Fascist Groove Thing by Heaven 17

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Music public relations expert Alan Edwards tells the story of why so many bands are getting back together, what happens when they do - and how such reunions have changed the music business. He starts by recalling the UK's first revival concert, which took place over 40 years ago at Wembley Stadium. Featuring Bill Hailey and the Comets, Little Richard and Jerry Lee Lewis - the event opened the eyes of promoters to the power of yesterday's hit-makers to reach an audience and make money. From there, Alan takes on a musical journey through some of the biggest reunions of the last 30 years. Contributors include Glen Matlock, Debbie Harry, Stewart Copeland and Melanie C

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Singer, DJ and broadcaster Cerys Matthews joins acclaimed blues photographer Val Wilmer to select their favourite blues musicians - several of whom Val has met and photographed. From Muddy Waters and Howlin' Wolf to Jimi Hendrix and John Lee Hooker, Sister Rosetta Tharpe to Peggy Lee and many more, their playlist is packed with classic blues and punctuated with personal stories

programme.title: Roy Orbison: Love Hurts
programme.desc: Documentary revealing Orbison's legacy as a beloved rock legend and a devoted father through intimate interviews with his three sons. Alex, Roy Jr and Wesley Orbison discuss the immense talent and fierce determination that provided the driving force behind their father's success and how his family life offered a strong spiritual base to escape the pressures of the rock 'n' roll lifestyle

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians take a trip on the Flying Scotsman, arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, along its original route connecting London and Edinburgh - the two most important financial capitals of the Empire. Peter Ginn explores how the railways revolutionised the delivery of mail across Britain, he and Alex Langlands learn about the first electronic communication service - more than 100 years before the arrival of email - and Ruth finds out about the role of the wheeltapper. Plus, why do some railway clocks have more than one minute hand?

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: In the village of Lekhani in Nepal, Michael watches potential Gurkha recruits being put through their paces. He then commences the 4,200m ascent to Annapurna Base Camp to help him acclimatise. Next he heads off to Kathmandu to fulfil a regal engagement at the king's palace during the sacred festival of Desain, and observes holy men at the Siva shrine of Pashupatinath

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: While dealing with the arrival of boatloads of migrants from across the Mediterranean - and with the harrowing human tragedy that accompanies them - the detective stumbles upon the murder of a dressmaker who had set up shop in Vigata. Montalbano must uncover the secrets behind the victim's life to get at the truth behind her death. Italian detective drama based on novels by Andrea Camilleri, starring Luca Zingaretti

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990

programme.title: Hip Hop: The Songs That Shook America

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead

programme.title: Victoria: A Royal Love Story
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce traces the monarch's love story by examining artefacts from a collection at the Queen's Gallery, Buckingham Palace. She looks at the paintings and sculptures she and Albert gave one another throughout their marriage, as well as meeting Prince Charles and visiting the couple's homes at Balmoral and on the Isle of Wight to understand more about their life together. Fiona also talks to the craftsmen restoring some of the objects today

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore charts Rome's rise from the abandonment and neglect of the 14th century into the everlasting seat of the Papacy recognised today. His story begins with the debauchery and decadence of the Renaissance, and takes viewers through the Catholic Reformation and on to the arrival of fascism and the creation of the Vatican State. He steps inside sensational palaces and churches, and talks about some of the world's most beloved art as he brings his exploration of Rome to a close

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: The team reaches Nepal, and journeys by tractor, local bus and dugout canoe to get to the Royal Chitwan National Park. Charley bonds with an elephant that takes him through remote Nepali villages, before reaching the capital Kathmandu. The adventurers also travel to Everest by helicopter and arrive in China, where they experience the breathtaking landscape of Yangshou from a hot-air balloon

programme.title: Britt-Marie Was Here (2019)
programme.desc: A sixtysomething former housewife starts work in the small town of Borg, where she is hired to coach the young soccer team. Comedy drama, starring Pernilla August, Vera Vitali and Peter Haber. In Swedish and German

programme.title: Mimi & Co
programme.desc: Mimi is on a mission to debunk stereotypes by showing that you don’t have to be just one thing in life. She celebrates people who inspire her by following their dreams and doing what they love. One of those people is Tesha, an old school friend who runs a salon, loves hair and make-up, but also plays football in the FA Women’s National League and internationally. We also meet Nora, who has albinism and is living her dream as a fashion model. And Mimi hosts her Q&A with SBTV creator Jamal Edwards MBE, who shares his own journey to success, and how he has helped the likes of Ed Sheeran. Mimi’s family and friends make a surprise appearance for the end of the series and offer words of wisdom for anyone wanting to chase their own dreams.

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Lauren Layfield hosts as two teams fight it out to dodge detention and put the cool back into school. In this episode, Mr Learner is training to be an art teacher and is working on his very own masterpiece. Trying to win Lauren’s shiny gold stars are team captains Harry and Ria, who are joined by CBBC’s Rhys Stephenson and comedians Lou Conran, Sam Fletcher and Bec Hill. They battle it out in a series of hilarious rounds guaranteed to make you laugh. Rounds include Stick to the Point, the word association game where teams are put on the spot by Lauren and her stick of pointiness. If there is any hesitation or stumbling, Lauren will put them in their ‘shush’ positions. In new game Boxymorons, teams have to try and guess the object hidden inside a box. And watch someone get pied in the face before we finish up on new game School Trip, where two of the team players board the school bus to answer questions.

Ollie is smuggled in to Lily's house by Martha to watch secretly from the attic, with Martha planning to split her time between both events. At the end of the make-over session Martha and Jas are horrified to see Lily has transformed herself to look exactly like Greta in a bid to impress Matt. programme.desc: A half-asleep Love Monster exits his house and walks towards the green, passing snoozing citizens on the way. He notices that the whole of the green is covered with litter and snoring Fluffytowners. Love Monster remembers that yesterday was Woo Hoo Day and the whole of Fluffytown had a big party - which makes today the Day After Woo Hoo Day. Love Monster attempts to clean up all of the mess on his own as a nice surprise for his friends, but he struggles and soon realises that it is a very big job. He should ask for help! He wakes everyone up, saying if everyone picks up one piece of litter each, they can tidy up the green in no time. Elder Kitten says he knows just the song for the job. Everyone takes their piece of litter to the recycling centre where they queue up to do their bit for the environment.

Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! programme.desc: In Italy, Mario, who's seven, spends Saturday cleaning prawns ready for customers at his dad's fish shop. On a hot day in Ghana, five-year-old Tyra cools off at the beach, and builds a massive sandcastle. And in Russia, seven-year-old Liza and her twin brother, Styopa, build a giant plastic tower, only to see it knocked down by their little sister Yulia. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and challenges of childhood are universal.

Maddie explains the different layers of our atmosphere and how astronauts travel through it into space and the special suits they wear to protect them. She tries on a real spacesuit and learns about the types of spacesuits they wear to do different jobs. Maddie discovers astronauts need to be fit, healthy and have good agility. She finds out how they learn how to move in space when they can’t keep their feet on the ground! programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.desc: The Squirrels spot strange dots with tails wriggling around in Duggie's new rock pool, and are confused to learn they are baby frogs

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In Vietnam, Truc takes pity on her lonely goldfish and goes shopping for some companions. In Russia, Liza and her twin brother, Styopa, take part in a craft class and make a lucky horseshoe for someone special. And in Jordan, Hamza makes a pizza for a picnic with his family in the local park

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?

programme.title: Our Family

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan meets a vet checking a dog's weight, and Rory Corrigan visits a vet at a rescue centre caring for British wildlife

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals

programme.title: Olobob Top

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out how space rockets work and how they take astronauts into space. We discover how thrust and rocket fuel work, when a rocket engine blasts off from the launch pad and into space, and learn about the different parts of the rocket and what they are for. Next, we find out about the special computers on-board that guide astronauts through space, and Maddie rides a very fast rollercoaster to show us how G-force works when a rocket launches. Finally, we find out how a rocket lands safely to bring the astronauts back to Earth.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is carefully putting the finishing touches to a set of cards that he is going to send out for Red Envelope Day. But after accidentally knocking into Delivery Duckling, the cards get mixed up, and he sends out Elder Kitten's envelope without his card in it! Love Monster must chase down Delivery Duckling and stop her from making the delivery.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Niger and Baby Bear meet babies Isobel, Idris, Lincoln, Amber, Amelia, Jason and Willow.

programme.title: Tinpo

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Tickbird wins a contest to see who can drink the most water in a single gulp, so Hippo allows her to sit on his back

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate explores different ways to travel. She uses an underwater camera to see a boat propeller and an animation reveals how the propeller pushes the boat through the water

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran

The game goes well, but JoJo doesn’t score any goals. Gran Gran reminds her she is good at catching and suggests she tries being a goalkeeper instead. With JoJo’s help, her team wins!

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

Later, when Rainer's latest batch of white wine is dyed blue, all fingers point to arch-rival Emilio Byass

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy takes action against Sharon, sending in the removal men and pleading with her to reveal what has happened to her son. Rainie confronts Max about his problems and tells him he is not fit to look after Abi. Mick goes to the police station to review his statement about Katy, while the truth comes out for Chelsea and Lucas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil looks out for Sharon on a tough day that sees her both celebrating Albie's first birthday and mourning Dennis's death. Kush and Whitney have no plans for Valentine's Day, so they watch a movie together at home - but when there is a power cut, they find themselves getting much closer. Stuart convinces Keegan to buy Tiffany a bag - but Rainie points out it is not the expensive handbag his wife wanted

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A stag do ends in tragedy when the host of a celebratory fishing trip is found harpooned on a beach the following morning. As the boat never moved, DI Neville Parker and his team struggle to determine how anyone in attendance could have been responsible. Caribbean-set detective drama, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Oscar-nominated Get Out star Daniel Kaluuya, who is appearing in crime biopic Judas and the Black Messiah. Hollywood star Sienna Miller talks about her surreal drama Wander Darkly, while Mayim Bialik chats about starring in new Miranda-based sitcom Call Me Kat. Hustle star Adrian Lester promotes the live-streaming new play Hymn, and comedians - and husband and wife - Marcus Brigstocke and Rachel Parris are appear. Plus, British singer-songwriter Arlo Parks performs her current single Caroline

programme.title: The Notebook (2004)
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about a passionate young couple whose fledgling affair is smothered almost before it has begun. Romantic drama, starring Ryan Gosling, Rachel McAdams, Gena Rowlands and James Garner

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the post-war British modern art movement in the seaside town of St Ives, the home for leading artists such as Barbara Hepworth and Terry Frost. At the Lizard Peninsula, Rick heads out to sea to fish for red mullet, and on the wild coast at Land's End, he discovers why Cornwall has a rather unusual animal as its national emblem

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: A group of dealers bid against each other for items collected by the public including two teddies, an Islamic tile, a painting and a chair brought in by a 12-year-old boy

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson-Bennett, former England fast-bowler Steve Harmison, world champion swimmer Karen Pickering and ex-Everton and Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Wales (Kick-off 4.45pm). All the action from the match in the second round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Scotland won in Wales for the first time in 18 years last season, prevailing 14-10 with the help of replacement hooker Stuart McInally's second-half try, and they will go into this match with all the more confidence after winning their opener away to England. However, the Welsh will be hoping to improve considerably this time around after a disappointing 2020 campaign that followed a Grand Slam the previous year, and got off to a good start with a victory at home to Ireland. Gabby Logan presents, with analysis from Jonathan Davies, Jeremy Guscott and John Barclay, commentary by Andrew Cotter, Sam Warburton and Chris Paterson, and pitchside reports from Andy Nicol

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a writers edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Jane Fallon, Fiona Neill, Nadia Shireen, Conn Iggulden, Richard Madeley, Allison Pearson, Tim Weaver and Jill Mansell

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: When Fenisha's life is in danger, Ethan must race against time to try and save his family. Meanwhile, Connie is determined to finally get things right with her daughter Grace, and Faith realises she has to put her family first, and decides to break off her arrangement with Dylan. Starring Olivia D'Lima, George Rainsford and Kirsty Mitchell

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Following the tragedy at the hospital, Fleming continues to work under cover in missing persons and looks into the disappearance of 15-year-old Carly Kirk, a case in which Denton appears to have a personal interest. The under-suspicion detective also starts to scrutinise the background of the AC-12 officers and gathers compromising evidence to use against the anti-corruption team

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur. The Reds recorded a comfortable 3-0 win when they met Leicester at Anfield in November, but the Foxes have enjoyed a strong campaign so far on the whole. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking demonstrate how much they had improved since a 2-0 defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium three months ago

programme.title: Sliding Doors (1998)
programme.desc: A London woman's entire future hinges on whether or not she catches a train. In one version of events, she gets the chance to start afresh and lead a happy new life, but in the other she is stuck supporting her lazy, unfaithful boyfriend. Comedy drama, presenting two parallel stories featuring the same character. Starring Gwyneth Paltrow, John Hannah and John Lynch

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in Suffolk with a north London couple who have a budget of £640,000, and also meets a goat farmer who made his own escape from the city to the Stour valley

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the recent meetings in Lievin and New York. With commentary by Steve Cram

programme.title: Monsters University (2013)
programme.desc: Student creatures Mike Wazowski and James P Sullivan, aka Sulley, attend university to learn how to be scary, but their rivalry gets them both kicked off the course. The monstrous duo must learn how to work together if they are to realise their dreams of terrifying children. Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.desc: Phil looks out for Sharon on a tough day that sees her both celebrating Albie's first birthday and mourning Dennis's death. Kush and Whitney have no plans for Valentine's Day, so they watch a movie together at home - but when there is a power cut, they find themselves getting much closer. Stuart convinces Keegan to buy Tiffany a bag - but Rainie points out it is not the expensive handbag his wife wanted

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A stag do ends in tragedy when the host of a celebratory fishing trip is found harpooned on a beach the following morning. As the boat never moved, DI Neville Parker and his team struggle to determine how anyone in attendance could have been responsible. Caribbean-set detective drama, starring Ralf Little and Josephine Jobert

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Oscar-nominated Get Out star Daniel Kaluuya, who is appearing in crime biopic Judas and the Black Messiah. programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson-Bennett, former England fast-bowler Steve Harmison, world champion swimmer Karen Pickering and ex-Everton and Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Joel Sked, Shaughan McGuigan, Craig Fowler and Robert Borthwick present a fun-filled look at Scottish football. The team members build their own on-field monster, analysing with scalpel-like precision to harvest the limbs of the Scotland's best players in creating their very own 'Frankenballer' and jostle for a spot in the A View from the Terrace 'Fives' team alongside four SPFL regulars

programme.title: The Notebook (2004)
programme.desc: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman in a nursing home, from an old notebook full of sentimental scribblings about a passionate young couple whose fledgling affair is smothered almost before it has begun. programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Wales (Kick-off 4.45pm). All the action from the match in the second round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Scotland won in Wales for the first time in 18 years last season, prevailing 14-10 with the help of replacement hooker Stuart McInally's second-half try, and they will go into this match with all the more confidence after winning their opener away to England. However, the Welsh will be hoping to improve considerably this time around after a disappointing 2020 campaign that followed a Grand Slam the previous year, and got off to a good start with a victory at home to Ireland. Gabby Logan presents, with analysis from Jonathan Davies, Jeremy Guscott and John Barclay, commentary by Andrew Cotter, Sam Warburton and Chris Paterson, and pitchside reports from Andy Nicol

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a writers edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: When Fenisha's life is in danger, Ethan must race against time to try and save his family. Meanwhile, Connie is determined to finally get things right with her daughter Grace, and Faith realises she has to put her family first, and decides to break off her arrangement with Dylan. Starring Olivia D'Lima, George Rainsford and Kirsty Mitchell

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Following the tragedy at the hospital, Fleming continues to work under cover in missing persons and looks into the disappearance of 15-year-old Carly Kirk, a case in which Denton appears to have a personal interest. The under-suspicion detective also starts to scrutinise the background of the AC-12 officers and gathers compromising evidence to use against the anti-corruption team

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur. The Reds recorded a comfortable 3-0 win when they met Leicester at Anfield in November, but the Foxes have enjoyed a strong campaign so far on the whole. Meanwhile, the Citizens were looking demonstrate how much they had improved since a 2-0 defeat at the hands of Spurs at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium three months ago

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents highlights from the Scottish Premiership, including Aberdeen v St Mirren, Rangers v Kilmarnock, and Ross County v Hibernian

programme.title: Sliding Doors (1998)
programme.desc: A London woman's entire future hinges on whether or not she catches a train. In one version of events, she gets the chance to start afresh and lead a happy new life, but in the other she is stuck supporting her lazy, unfaithful boyfriend. Comedy drama, presenting two parallel stories featuring the same character. Starring Gwyneth Paltrow, John Hannah and John Lynch

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Liverpool and Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts moral and religious discussions on whether Britain is still racist, and would the world be a better place if everyone believed in reincarnation

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: The Rev Aftab Gohar of Abbotsgrange Parish Church in Grangemouth and the Rev Sarah Shaw of Christ Church in Falkirk present the religious programme

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters visit a grand Edwardian home in Eltham, south-east London, a piece of land in Handsworth, Birmingham, and a property in Newcastle-under-Lyme in Staffordshire

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits Ripon Cathedral to hear reflections on Lent, and Katie Piper meets a Christian in Leeds who donated a kidney to a stranger

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall where she sifts through the junk in search of two items that can be transformed. Upholsterer Ray Clarke transforms a traditional wing-back chair and there's a surprise in store with a rug Sarah found that could mean some cash to return to its unsuspecting owner

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley is in Suffolk with a north London couple who have a budget of £640,000, and also meets a goat farmer who made his own escape from the city to the Stour valley

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the recent meetings in Lievin and New York. With commentary by Steve Cram

programme.title: Monsters University (2013)
programme.desc: Student creatures Mike Wazowski and James P Sullivan, aka Sulley, attend university to learn how to be scary, but their rivalry gets them both kicked off the course. The monstrous duo must learn how to work together if they are to realise their dreams of terrifying children. Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is at Kentish Town City farm to lend a helping hand as well as taking a look back through the programme's archives to celebrate winter heroes, from the farmers toughing it out in all weathers to members of a community working together to save the heart of their village. Margherita also catches up with one of her childhood heroes in former Olympic ski jumper Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Bodnant Garden, a National Trust property near Tal-y-Cafn in Conwy, where items assessed include an ecclesiastical candlestick from a very special cathedral and some novelty glass bottles with a hefty price tag. There is also a tandem bicycle and sidecar and a selection of 20th-century automata

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: The net begins to close on Charles and Marie-Andree in India,and he hatches a daring plan to escape to South America, but he is becoming reckless and soon everything spirals out of control. In Bangkok, Herman, Angela and the Action Committee face up to life after the Sobhraj case

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Arsenal v Leeds United at Emirates Stadium, West Bromwich Albion v Manchester United at The Hawthorns, Southampton v Wolverhampton Wanderers at St Mary's Stadium and Everton v Fulham at Goodison Park. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Highlights of St Johnstone v Celtic at McDiarmid Park, plus a round-up of Saturday's matches in the Scottish Premiership

programme.title: A Royal Team Talk: Tackling Mental Health
programme.desc: The Duke of Cambridge, Gareth Southgate, Peter Crouch, Thierry Henry, Danny Rose and Jermaine Jenas gather together in the company of Dan Walker for an extraordinary and revealing conversation about men's mental health

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin explore properties in Worcestershire, South Croydon and Edgbaston, and follow investors hoping to make cash from their auction buys

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Michael Goodinson firmly believes his family's future lies in New Zealand, but wife Lisa sees things very differently. With major health concerns, time is not on Michael's side, so the clan embarks on a trial week sampling the life they could have 'Down Under'. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Ashford, Kent, a report of a burglary ends with a police officer fighting a man armed with a knife. Plus, other officers in the county get to grips with taser training

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Presenter Eric Knowles and experts Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique-hunting in Wrexham, north Wales, before heading to Stockport for the auction. Eric is also faced with a mystery where the answer might be under his nose

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King visit beaches and mountains in Thailand and discover how the country's multicultural history and its place at the heart of the spice trade have helped create a rich and varied cuisine. They taste a traditional Thai breakfast and cook their own jungle curry for a remote hill tribe

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max head out into wine country to investigate the killing of a dog at one of the island's most famous vineyards - Bodegas Negra. It transpires that the shooting is just one of an increasingly dramatic series of threats faced by property owner Hans Webber, his daughter Yvonne and son-in-law Rainer. Later, when Rainer's latest batch of white wine is dyed blue, all fingers point to arch-rival Emilio Byass

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to Dorset to help relocate a retired doctor and her husband who are selling up their Surrey family home. They have an £800,000 budget to spend on a spacious country property where they can enjoy their respective hobbies. Jonnie also heads out into the waters of West Bay to see how a conservation scheme is helping protect healthy levels of marine life and the livelihoods of local fishermen

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: International sheepdog breeder David and farmer Brenda battle the temperamental British weather with their pedigree dogs by their sides, competing in Powburn, Northumberland, at one of Britain's oldest sheepdog trials

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more sellers to the Bidding Room, where they try to sell items to five professional dealers who are on the lookout for interesting pieces, with help from expert Simon Bower. Here, the dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage stereoscope, two gigantic apothecary glasses and a children's game thought to be from the 19th century

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the week with a lively mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson-Bennett, former England fast-bowler Steve Harmison, world champion swimmer Karen Pickering and ex-Everton and Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy takes action against Sharon, sending in the removal men and pleading with her to reveal what has happened to her son. programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: The Exiles: Hong Kong at a Crossroads
programme.desc: In June 2020, after months of increasingly violent protests in Hong Kong, Beijing imposed a sweeping national security law. Critics say it effectively curtails protest and freedom of speech - China has said it will return stability. In response, the UK has introduced a new visa that will give 70% of its former colony's population - 5.4m people - the right to live in the UK, and eventually become citizens. Many activists now face an agonising choice - to stay or to go.

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Lucy Hedges looks at Dubai's efforts to reschedule its mammoth 2020 World Expo, which was postponed during the pandemic. The team also catches up with Cuba's enigmatic shoeshine poet, checks out the typewriter tycoon whose fortunes changed one part of northern Italy and meets the South Korean robot that can peer into the future.
