programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: With schools, colleges and universities around the world closed by Covid-19, the pandemic is blowing a hole in the lives of young people, their education and their life prospects. Remote learning can accentuate the differences between the haves and the have-nots. Time out of school can also mean a lack of decent food for the poorest and increase the risk of domestic violence against girls. Higher education has been thrown into disarray, with those on the cusp of graduation entering an uncertain job market. How can we stop this cruel disruption to young lives becoming a global generational disaster?

programme.title: Cearcall
programme.desc: Donald Morrison introduces vintage reports from Wester Ross and Lochalsh. Plus, a report on the Highland Fund and some of the districts helped by it

programme.title: Air Iomall-Music from the Edge
programme.desc: Two of Scotland's leading young traditional musicians, Charlie Grey and Joseph Peach, take a musical voyage on the Dutch vessel Wylde Swan to some of the most remote islands off Scotland's north-west coast

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy presents a show with folk and traditional music from Celtic Connections. This programme features a cross section of music, including Kris Drever and band, Dallahan, Rhiannon Giddens and Josie Duncan and her band

programme.title: Concorde
programme.desc: Part two of two. The makers, pilots and passengers tell the story of how an Anglo-French team saw off competition from the US and USSR to build the world's only supersonic airliner

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming, heading to the coastal region of Sanna, at the far western tip of the peninsula of Ardnamurchan, in Lochaber

programme.title: Scottish Premiership Football
programme.desc: Motherwell v Hamilton Academical. Full delayed coverage of the SPL match at Fir Park. Commentary from Alex O'Henley

programme.title: ALT
programme.desc: Including a live performance from Astrid, harpist Rachel Newton sings a Gaelic song accompanied by fiddle and percussion, and Eddi Reader chats about her love of Robert Burns

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: Seren the bat shows how good she is at counting while in search of a scale

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Sid wants to give Penny a present

programme.title: Rita is Crogall
programme.desc: Rita and Crocodile spend the day on the beach finding lots of shells and beautiful pebbles

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: A new family is moving into the flat, a mother and her son named Zizou. Perhaps he would like to become friends with Huggleboo

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Everybody is really excited today as, while building a playhouse, Bing, Sula and Pando find a bird’s nest with eggs inside

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is not at all happy when Pando calls him 'Biongalaidh Bongalaidh' all the time but after a while, he learns that songs with silly words are fun sometimes

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda
programme.desc: Shifu's father visits the Jade Palace, but Po is disappointed to learn that he and his son do not get along

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Everything goes like clockwork until Coinneach Clogaid arrives on the scene - and the cuckoo agrees with this wholeheartedly

programme.title: Ronia, Nighean a' Mheirlich/Ronja, the Robber's Daughter
programme.desc: Mata and his robbers are trying to drive Borca and his men out of the fort and Ronia ia surprised that she hasn’t seen Biorc for quite some time

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Sìm is deaf, but he never lets that get in the way of mischief making, and he's delighted to be spending the holidays with his granny

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Seachd Là (News Review)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents a mix of hymns, psalms and faith stories. Plus, Glasgow Gaelic Musical Association sing Molaibh Dia, and Niall MacLennan and Alasdair MacPhee perform Psalm 96, Isabel MacLeod shares the role faith plays in her life and Angela MacEachen reads from the Book of Proverbs

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Annabel Maclennan presents a selection of Eòrpa reports from previous years connected to Europe's coasts and seas. The programme includes an account of how migrants make the perilous sea crossing to Greece to seek a better life, a report on a controversial plan to build a canal near Poland's border with Russia, and how record low water levels in the River Danube were affecting Hungary's tourism industry

programme.title: An Taigh-Fuine/The Bakery
programme.desc: Martin prepares hot cross buns, and in the restaurant in Balivanich, the family pull together as they prepare for a very important annual event - Mother's Day

programme.title: A' Suathadh Ri Iomadh Rubha
programme.desc: On his death in 2015, Norman Campbell had written published many novels and children's books in Gaelic. He remains one of the 20th century's most respected Gaelic writers. This documentary reveals not just details of Norman's work, but also that of his father and uncle - and of the achievements of his children in the arts

programme.title: Seirm
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy presents a show with folk and traditional music from Celtic Connections. This programme features a cross section of music, including Kris Drever and band, Dallahan, Rhiannon Giddens and Josie Duncan and her band

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Ingrid plans a party for Fredrik's 50th birthday and breaks social rules to support a friend in trouble. Christian and the other divers at the Ocean Viking are taking risks every day at work, where things are getting increasingly dangerous. In Norwegian and English

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Kris Drever's solo performance, including ‘Freedom Come A' Ye', from Na Trads in 2019

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Married couple Sandeep and Judd, Wirral sisters Nicola and Pamela, and Tamworth couple Karen and Ben use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing. Hosted by Adil Ray

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Donnie, Kate, Jack and Nicole work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chas has a proposition for Marlon, Paul is on edge, and Mandy is troubled by the feeling that something is not right

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin and Debbie get trapped in the Bistro fridge, and Johnny tells Carla that the doctor is referring him to a psychiatrist. Tyrone realises he has been duped while looking after the furniture shop for Fiz, and Leanne books a consultation with a TV psychic

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist, whose body has been dumped in a Northumberland landfill site, surroundings that make evidence of how she died impossible to verify. Vera discovers that the dead woman had been working for the prison service re-investigating a crime that a former prisoner had been convicted of, and realises she must finish what the victim started in order to discover who may have wanted her dead. Paul Kaye plays a new pathologist alongside fellow guest star Peter Davison

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gives six new contestants the chance to take their place in the hot seat. Will any of them have what it takes to become a million-pound winner?

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the semi-final of the singing contest, in which regular panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan are joined by special guest judge Matt Lucas. The show will feature a double elimination, as two of the five remaining contestants will be unmasked and depart the contest

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: A short bulletin rounding up the day's entertainment stories

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Murtaugh turns to Tom Barnes for help on a high-stakes cartel case, while he and Trish meet Natalie and Maya for the first time. Tensions mount between Bailey and 'The Gute' as the new partners begin to work together

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: After hearing how Dana's parents fell in love, Dana helps a loneley Sinornithosaurus impress a female

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: George adapts the spin cycle of the washing machine causing a black hole to form in the Spuds' kitchen.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Gran needs new teeth. Probably not wise to nab a set found on the side of the road though, under a full moon.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: When two space freighters crash in low-earth orbit, Alan and John must evacuate the hot-headed pilots from a dangerous debris field

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny takes on one of the UK's toughest obstacle courses, but can he beat the clock and avoid the forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The bumbling buffoon decides that acquiring some pets could liven up his dull existence. Animated comedy, with the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A day on the beach turns into a desperate struggle to catch the sun for Mr Bean

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Tom and Jerry must prevent the entire city blowing up

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Mavis' friends from her old Ghoul Guide troop come to the hotel for a visit

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the Kids agree to escort an airship shipment of food to a drought-torn region of China, but Nu Hai has some suspicions about Shi Long that she finds aren't far off when she discovers that the airship is set to explode with them on board!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guests Anton Du Beke, Mark Tilling and John Hooker, and serves them up a feast of chicken and chorizo and panna cotta. Anton chats about his latest book, Mark makes some special Valentine's Day chocolates, and John shares his unusual recipe for duck

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef reflects on how the British food scene has changed since he first came to the UK over 30 years ago. He whips up a pea soup, stuffed peppers, and his own take on brown sauce. He is joined by Paul Ainsworth, who prepares an alternative cheese board

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef ventures into Salem on the east coast of the USA, a place made famous by the witch trials of 1692. Things get spooky as James visits on the approach to Halloween and he meets a love clairvoyant, and her dogs

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: England v Italy (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the opening match in the second round of fixtures, held at Twickenham. England have won all 27 of the previous matches between the sides, including a 34-5 triumph at Stadio Olimpico in last season's competition, when Eddie Jones' men won the championship and the Italians finished bottom of the table once again. Both teams will be looking for improved performances this time out, having suffered home defeats against Scotland and France respectively. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield, Maggie Alphonsi and Michele Campagnaro, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as comedian Joe Lycett, Pussycat Doll Melody Thornton and retired cricket commentator Henry Blofeld take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with contestants Vick Hope, Ranvir Singh and Iain Stirling all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the final of the singing contest in which the panel and the studio audience must guess the last three famous faces behind the elaborate costumes. Regular panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan are joined by last year's winner Nicola Roberts, while all 12 of this year's masked singers will be returning for a grand finale performance, as unmasked celebrities Sophie Ellis-Bextor, Mel B, Glenn Hoddle, Martine McCutcheon, John Thomson, Lenny Henry, Morten Harket and last week's semi-finalists don their costumes once more

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final round of this year's blind auditions, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The coaches will be judging tonight's contestants on their voice alone, listening out for that perfect performance that inspires them to spin their chairs and pick one more act for their team

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Josie d'Arby, Emilia Fox, Richard Madeley and John Thomson to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: GoldenEye (1995)
programme.desc: James Bond heads for Russia to investigate the connection between the theft of an experimental helicopter and an orbital satellite weapon with the power to devastate entire cities. He discovers both have fallen into the hands of a mysterious criminal mastermind with a grudge against Britain and a connection with 007's past. Spy adventure, with Pierce Brosnan taking on his first assignment as 007. Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco and Famke Janssen also star. Directed by Martin Campbell, who later helmed Daniel Craig's first outing as Bond, Casino Royale

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature, alongside the legendary trolley dashes

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Dino Dana
programme.desc: The palaeontologist-in-training enlists a pack of Troodon to help Ms Currie locate her grandson's lost pug

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: George wins tickets to the local stately home. It's not long before Dave and the rest of the Spuds find themselves lost in its maze.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Summer rears its hot, cheeky head in Grimsby, but Dave has found a way to keep the Spud flat nice and cool

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A former disaster specialist comes out of retirement to help Virgil and Gordon cap a leaking gas reserve

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's dare is to drive a tank blindfolded. Can he get across the course and knock down the cardboard box wall?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean goes on a date with his girlfriend, complete with romantic movie and candlelit dinner

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The popular halfwit tries to be romantic, but his idea of what that means proves to be very different to Irma's. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Following a bump to the head, Mercury seems to be acting like a dog, much to Mike's surprise

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Batgirl's worlds collide when her best friend from Gotham City moves to Metropolis.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter hikes seven and a half miles of the wild Lizard Peninsula, Cornwall's most southerly point, starting at the much-photographed Kynance Cove and finishing at the idyllic fishing village of Cadgwith. Along the way she learns about Serpentine Rock, visits a hut that changed the course of modern technology, and gets a pasty making master class

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh is joined by Wendy Craig, Will Kirk and Frank Gardner

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef reflects on how the British food scene has changed since he first came to the UK over 30 years ago. He whips up a pea soup, stuffed peppers, and his own take on brown sauce. He is joined by Paul Ainsworth, who prepares an alternative cheese board

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team heads to the Yorkshire Dales to surprise Jim Thompson, a terminally ill 65-year-old grandfather and fundraiser. Despite Jim's own diagnosis, his commitment to spreading awareness and raising money for prostate cancer charities are legendary in the area, including creating a 'Calendar Girls' style naked calendar. Inspired by his bravery and dedication, the makeover team decide to turn Jim's overgrown and dangerous plot into a classic cottage garden with a Yorkshire twist

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v France (Kick-off 3.00pm). Jill Douglas presents coverage of the final match in the second round of fixtures, held at Aviva Stadium. France overcame Ireland 35-27 in Paris in the concluding contest of last year's competition, but were unable to secure the bonus point and 31-point margin required to prevent England winning the title, while Ireland could have topped the table had they recorded a bonus and prevailed by seven points or more. With analysis by Brian O'Driscoll, Rory Best and Gareth Thomas, and commentary by Miles Harrison, David Flatman and Geordan Murphy

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: This week it's The Greatest Love Stories On Ice as the remaining celebrities and their professional partners battle to stay in the competition. Holly Willoughby and Phillip Schofield host, while Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman. Plus, a guest performance by Rita Ora, who performs her new single, Bang Bang

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Host Jeremy Clarkson may also lend a hand, if the players trust his general knowledge

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Alice decides to reject Tanvi's offer - Harry would have wanted Alice to develop the land herself with the support of their family. But it's not long before the land project is overshadowed, as Nicola blurts out that Alice is also hoping to try for Harry's baby using the sperm that he mysteriously stored before he died. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The duo continue their European adventure in Italy, starting in the picturesque Lake Garda, where Barney surprises his dad by explaining they'll be running off the top of Monte Baldo and paragliding over the lake below. The next day Bradley is treated to a hair-raising ride in a Formula 1 speed boat, before the Walshes are invited to the home of opera superstar Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Claire Martin was abandoned in Hong Kong as a baby more than 50 years ago and heads back there to appeal for information about her origins, but with the help of DNA she finds family much closer to home. The team helps Nicki Goscomb and her sisters search for their brother, who was given up for adoption in 1966

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Married couple Sandeep and Judd, Wirral sisters Nicola and Pamela, and Tamworth couple Karen and Ben use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing. Hosted by Adil Ray

programme.desc: The chef reflects on how the British food scene has changed since he first came to the UK over 30 years ago. He whips up a pea soup, stuffed peppers, and his own take on brown sauce. He is joined by Paul Ainsworth, who prepares an alternative cheese board

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef ventures into Salem on the east coast of the USA, a place made famous by the witch trials of 1692. Things get spooky as James visits on the approach to Halloween and he meets a love clairvoyant, and her dogs

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: England v Italy (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the opening match in the second round of fixtures, held at Twickenham. England have won all 27 of the previous matches between the sides, including a 34-5 triumph at Stadio Olimpico in last season's competition, when Eddie Jones' men won the championship and the Italians finished bottom of the table once again. Both teams will be looking for improved performances this time out, having suffered home defeats against Scotland and France respectively. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward and Lawrence Dallaglio, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield, Maggie Alphonsi and Michele Campagnaro, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and Ugo Monye

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as comedian Joe Lycett, Pussycat Doll Melody Thornton and retired cricket commentator Henry Blofeld take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with contestants Vick Hope, Ranvir Singh and Iain Stirling all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the final of the singing contest in which the panel and the studio audience must guess the last three famous faces behind the elaborate costumes. Regular panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan are joined by last year's winner Nicola Roberts, while all 12 of this year's masked singers will be returning for a grand finale performance, as unmasked celebrities Sophie Ellis-Bextor, Mel B, Glenn Hoddle, Martine McCutcheon, John Thomson, Lenny Henry, Morten Harket and last week's semi-finalists don their costumes once more

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final round of this year's blind auditions, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The coaches will be judging tonight's contestants on their voice alone, listening out for that perfect performance that inspires them to spin their chairs and pick one more act for their team

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Josie d'Arby, Emilia Fox, Richard Madeley and John Thomson to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: GoldenEye (1995)
programme.desc: James Bond heads for Russia to investigate the connection between the theft of an experimental helicopter and an orbital satellite weapon with the power to devastate entire cities. He discovers both have fallen into the hands of a mysterious criminal mastermind with a grudge against Britain and a connection with 007's past. Spy adventure, with Pierce Brosnan taking on his first assignment as 007. Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco and Famke Janssen also star. Directed by Martin Campbell, who later helmed Daniel Craig's first outing as Bond, Casino Royale

programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter hikes seven and a half miles of the wild Lizard Peninsula, Cornwall's most southerly point, starting at the much-photographed Kynance Cove and finishing at the idyllic fishing village of Cadgwith. Along the way she learns about Serpentine Rock, visits a hut that changed the course of modern technology, and gets a pasty making master class

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh is joined by Wendy Craig, Will Kirk and Frank Gardner

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef reflects on how the British food scene has changed since he first came to the UK over 30 years ago. He whips up a pea soup, stuffed peppers, and his own take on brown sauce. He is joined by Paul Ainsworth, who prepares an alternative cheese board

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team heads to the Yorkshire Dales to surprise Jim Thompson, a terminally ill 65-year-old grandfather and fundraiser. Despite Jim's own diagnosis, his commitment to spreading awareness and raising money for prostate cancer charities are legendary in the area, including creating a 'Calendar Girls' style naked calendar. Inspired by his bravery and dedication, the makeover team decide to turn Jim's overgrown and dangerous plot into a classic cottage garden with a Yorkshire twist

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v France (Kick-off 3.00pm). Jill Douglas presents coverage of the final match in the second round of fixtures, held at Aviva Stadium. France overcame Ireland 35-27 in Paris in the concluding contest of last year's competition, but were unable to secure the bonus point and 31-point margin required to prevent England winning the title, while Ireland could have topped the table had they recorded a bonus and prevailed by seven points or more. With analysis by Brian O'Driscoll, Rory Best and Gareth Thomas, and commentary by Miles Harrison, David Flatman and Geordan Murphy

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: This week it's The Greatest Love Stories On Ice as the remaining celebrities and their professional partners battle to stay in the competition. Holly Willoughby and Phillip Schofield host, while Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman. Plus, a guest performance by Rita Ora, who performs her new single, Bang Bang

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Host Jeremy Clarkson may also lend a hand, if the players trust his general knowledge

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: Alice decides to reject Tanvi's offer - Harry would have wanted Alice to develop the land herself with the support of their family. But it's not long before the land project is overshadowed, as Nicola blurts out that Alice is also hoping to try for Harry's baby using the sperm that he mysteriously stored before he died. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The duo continue their European adventure in Italy, starting in the picturesque Lake Garda, where Barney surprises his dad by explaining they'll be running off the top of Monte Baldo and paragliding over the lake below. The next day Bradley is treated to a hair-raising ride in a Formula 1 speed boat, before the Walshes are invited to the home of opera superstar Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Claire Martin was abandoned in Hong Kong as a baby more than 50 years ago and heads back there to appeal for information about her origins, but with the help of DNA she finds family much closer to home. The team helps Nicki Goscomb and her sisters search for their brother, who was given up for adoption in 1966

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Married couple Sandeep and Judd, Wirral sisters Nicola and Pamela, and Tamworth couple Karen and Ben use their powers of deduction to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show to potentially double their winnings, or leaving with nothing. Hosted by Adil Ray

programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Aunt Joan reveals she has a rare bird nesting on her land but wants to keep it a secret to deter prospective egg thieves. Sure enough, her tactless nephew opens his mouth to the local baker, who spies an opportunity to save his ailing business by making it a new attraction. Before long the peaceful community of Portwenn is over-run by twitchers eager to catch a sight of the bird, and Dr Ellingham finds himself in the locals' bad books yet again. Martin Clunes stars in this Christmas special of the comedy drama, originally shown in 2006

Crime drama set in the 1940s, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Tamzin Outhwaite and Rupert Vansittart

With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Leanne lies to Nick, making out that Imran's keeping her job open for her and she is fine for money. Having failed to secure a mortgage, David and Shona are at their wit's end. Johnny is horrified when he fails to recognise a cry for help from Joe, a suicidal fellow prisoner

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby decides to join Causton's annual trip to Brighton when he becomes suspicious about the mayor's plans to buy up coastal land for holiday chalets. But before he can get to the bottom of the dubious deal, a hated property developer is beheaded at a funfair and a collection of valuable historical swords goes missing. With the help of his cousin DCI John Barnaby, the detective sets out to crack the case. Drama, guest starring Neil Dudgeon (Roman's Empire), Mark Gatiss (The League of Gentlemen), Coronation Street villain Brian Capron and Blackadder's Tim McInnerny

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the mysterious death of businessman John Shearwood, found floating in the water under the Gateshead Millennium Bridge when he was supposed to be on a golfing trip to Ireland. The man's wife Stella and brother Luke are baffled to discover he was working as an informant for HM Revenue and Customs. Chief Revenue investigator Owen Preece (guest star Robert Glenister) reveals John's business manager Mark Donovan has been running an alcohol scam through their haulage firm and the deceased had agreed to offer information in exchange for immunity from prosecution. Was his cover blown? Owen doesn't think so, and Mark has an alibi - unlike John's wife. Brenda Blethyn stars, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: An anonymous enemy goes all out to ruin Wycliffe by sending the police a batch of photos apparently showing him taking bribes. As soon as she hears the news, Lane puts an investigation into the deaths of two drug-dealers on hold and leaps to her boss's defence - risking her career in the process. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Staff at the London hotel prepare for Valentine's Day, one of the busiest and most profitable nights of the year. Downstairs in the florists, boss Belinda and junior florist MaryKate prepare £20,000 worth of flowers, which will be decorated across the hotel throughout the week

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: When an unpopular pub landlord is beaten to death, a police informant casts suspicion on his ex-wife and her new husband. programme.desc: On their eviction day, Zak and Butch paint their house pink. Paddy's mum insults the Dingles, leading the vet to defend them. Chris believes he has figured out Kim's plan

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accountant and his family crash into a tractor after being chased down a country lane by another car. Meanwhile, David buys an old Jeep with a surprising military history. Starring Joe McFadden

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid wants a lie-in, but it is doomed from the beginning as Sally chases romance and Jean goes on a diet. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: A valuable heirloom brought back from the China Sea could change the Ropers' fortunes - but not if Mildred's sister gets it first. Starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A flood of forged banknotes leads the police to believe a fugitive gangster has arrived in Aidensfield, but their efforts to flush him out put Dawson in peril. Meanwhile, Miller tries to remain professional while dealing with his relationship problems and Bernie thinks his favourite celebrity has moved in down the road. Gemma Craven guest stars, with Clare Wille, John Duttine, Peter Benson and Joe McFadden

programme.desc: Chris goes to the police with his theory about Kim and Steve. Paddy and Mandy discuss their relationship. DI Spalding interviews Kim about the robbery

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily sees Don at the cemetery, visiting Ivy's grave. Martin gets angry when Mike accuses him of helping Brennan escape custody. Ken discovers Jon is not who he claims to be

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fred locks himself away from the neighbours. Mavis and Rita fall out over Ms Wilton's leaving party. Don tells Mike he intends to kill him, but wants to hear him beg first

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Aunt Joan reveals she has a rare bird nesting on her land but wants to keep it a secret to deter prospective egg thieves. Sure enough, her tactless nephew opens his mouth to the local baker, who spies an opportunity to save his ailing business by making it a new attraction. Before long the peaceful community of Portwenn is over-run by twitchers eager to catch a sight of the bird, and Dr Ellingham finds himself in the locals' bad books yet again. Martin Clunes stars in this Christmas special of the comedy drama, originally shown in 2006

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into the strange murder of eminent art historian Professor Van Haren, who was really a former high-ranking Nazi hired by MI5 due to his expert knowledge of Soviet intelligence. Meanwhile, as Sam's husband Adam embarks on a busy career as an MP, she worries whether she will be able to share some big news with him. Crime drama set in the 1940s, starring Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks, with Tamzin Outhwaite and Rupert Vansittart

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler

programme.desc: The detective duo pose as Middle Eastern terrorists in the search for an arms dealer reputed to be supplying weapons to violent groups in Britain. Glynis Barber stars

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The murders of several British agents seem to be the work of an infamous spy whom Steed killed years previously. Julian Glover guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur comes up with a plan to boost business at the Winchester Club by staging variety nights. The idea proves popular until the karaoke machine, rented from the fearsome Gabadini family, is stolen. George Cole stars

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole takes a wild ride through Gran Canaria, and visits the Morgan factory to learn about their iconic three wheelers and what they are like to ride. The presenters also track down a rare 1970s Chopper to restore

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The great detective reveals to a startled Watson how he defeated Moriarty and cheated death beneath the waters of the Reichenbach Falls. Period drama, marking Edward Hardwicke's debut as Holmes' indefatigable chronicler

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Drug dealers kidnap a woman's child in a bid to deter her from testifying against them at a forthcoming - forcing Dempsey to turn to shady character the Bogeyman for help

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is assigned to ensure the safe shipment of a gold bullion consignment, threatened by the plans of a brilliant computer expert bent on revenge. Drama, guest starring George Cole and John Hurt, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Britain's most advanced computer suffers a series of acts of sabotage. Steed and Tara try to decode a cryptic message from the machine naming the culprit. Dennis Price guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur dabbles in the world of nouvelle cuisine, taking bookings on banquets. However, his chef goes on the run from a group of gangsters leaving him in the lurch when catering for a policeman's retirement party. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole's Mediterranean odyssey continues with a ride through southern Spain. Back home, the restoration of the 1970 Chopper gathers pace, and Henry also gets a sneak peak at the latest models from AJS

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five members of a Solihull cricket club, team up to take on a series of top 10 list questions. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Good Morning Britain's Ranvir Singh, journalist John Sergeant, ex-rugby international Ugo Monye and TV presenter Mariella Frostrup pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

Thriller sequel, starring Roy Scheider, Lorraine Gary and Murray Hamilton

programme.desc: A solicitor enlists Holmes to help clear him of murdering eccentric builder Jonas Oldacre, whose considerable personal fortune he stood to inherit. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett as the famed Victorian detective

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A doctor visits Holmes on behalf of his friend and sponsor Blessington, a nervous invalid whose fragile peace of mind has been thrown into turmoil by a series of mysterious events. Drama, guest starring Patrick Newell and Nicholas Clay, with Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes

Gabriel Clarke introduces coverage of the 1979 FA Cup final at the old Wembley Stadium, which featured the likes of Pat Jennings and Liam Brady for the Gunners, and Joe Jordan and Jimmy Greenhoff for United. programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In a remote part of the Amazon, a corpse has been recovered bearing extensive and unusual bruising and Jeremy Wade hears further reports of a terrifying predator that cannot be caught with a rod and line. programme.title: World of Sport

With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Nevada Smith (1966)
programme.desc: The son of a Native American mother and a white father dedicates his life to gaining revenge on the three outlaws who murdered them. Western adventure, starring Steve McQueen, Karl Malden, Brian Keith, Arthur Kennedy and Suzanne Pleshette

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The Jager family boat hits the water after being grounded for four months. Skipper Glenn needs money, and he's taking his son with him, but a predator puts their haul in doubt. Plus, Squizzy is on a four-day run to restock his mobile lobster van, but rough seas and the market crisis leave him emotional

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The concrete boat job has turned into a nightmare. The houseboat has collapsed in on itself and there's mess and concrete debris everywhere. While Luke, Jayde and Ellie navigate the extreme humidity, mud and mangroves, Dan and Jordan are off to evaluate their next job. The Belle Canto is smashed upon the rocks. It's a total write-off and they wont be able to use Christopher to winch it out. To make matters worse, Jordan has lost control of the team's dingy.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Moriarty seeks revenge on Holmes for foiling his plan to sell the Mona Lisa, and the pair ultimately fight their final battle at the Reichenbach Falls in Switzerland. Jeremy Brett stars as the titular hero, with David Burke making his final appearance as Dr Watson and Eric Porter as Moriarty

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The great detective reveals to a startled Watson how he defeated Moriarty and cheated death beneath the waters of the Reichenbach Falls. Period drama, marking Edward Hardwicke's debut as Holmes' indefatigable chronicler

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: First episode of the drama based on CS Forester's tales of an 18th-century seafaring adventurer. The 17-year-old Horatio Hornblower experiences seasickness while still in Spithead as he begins his Navy career and runs into trouble with a tormentor - until war with France gives him a chance to prove himself in the heat of battle. Ioan Gruffudd and Robert Lindsay star

programme.title: Apollo 13 (1995)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the ill-fated 1970 Apollo 13 mission to the moon. Crew leader Jim Lovell is faced with a nightmare when the mission suffers an on-board explosion - leaving the team stranded in space with a dwindling oxygen supply while flight controllers and engineers in Houston try to find a way to bring the crew safely back to Earth. With Tom Hanks, Kevin Bacon, Bill Paxton, Ed Harris and Gary Sinise

programme.title: Captain Newman MD (1964)
programme.desc: As an American Army psychiatrist stationed on an airbase during the final months of World War the Second World War faces a constant battle with bureaucracy as the army fails to recognise the seriousness of his patients' conditions. Fortunately, he has a resourceful corporal on hand to beg, borrow or steal much-needed resources. Comedy drama, starring Gregory Peck, Tony Curtis, Bobby Darin and Angie Dickinson

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The murders of several British agents seem to be the work of an infamous spy whom Steed killed years previously. Julian Glover guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur comes up with a plan to boost business at the Winchester Club by staging variety nights. The idea proves popular until the karaoke machine, rented from the fearsome Gabadini family, is stolen. George Cole stars

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole takes a wild ride through Gran Canaria, and visits the Morgan factory to learn about their iconic three wheelers and what they are like to ride. The presenters also track down a rare 1970s Chopper to restore

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The great detective reveals to a startled Watson how he defeated Moriarty and cheated death beneath the waters of the Reichenbach Falls. Period drama, marking Edward Hardwicke's debut as Holmes' indefatigable chronicler

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Drug dealers kidnap a woman's child in a bid to deter her from testifying against them at a forthcoming - forcing Dempsey to turn to shady character the Bogeyman for help

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is assigned to ensure the safe shipment of a gold bullion consignment, threatened by the plans of a brilliant computer expert bent on revenge. Drama, guest starring George Cole and John Hurt, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Britain's most advanced computer suffers a series of acts of sabotage. Steed and Tara try to decode a cryptic message from the machine naming the culprit. Dennis Price guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur dabbles in the world of nouvelle cuisine, taking bookings on banquets. However, his chef goes on the run from a group of gangsters leaving him in the lurch when catering for a policeman's retirement party. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole's Mediterranean odyssey continues with a ride through southern Spain. Back home, the restoration of the 1970 Chopper gathers pace, and Henry also gets a sneak peak at the latest models from AJS

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five members of a Solihull cricket club, team up to take on a series of top 10 list questions. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Good Morning Britain's Ranvir Singh, journalist John Sergeant, ex-rugby international Ugo Monye and TV presenter Mariella Frostrup pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Jaws 2 (1978)
programme.desc: The seaside resort of Amity is terrorised once again by a man-eating great white shark. While police chief Brody struggles to alert the townsfolk to the magnitude of the danger, a group of teenagers - including Brody's two sons - is adrift at sea in waters hunted by the shark. Thriller sequel, starring Roy Scheider, Lorraine Gary and Murray Hamilton

programme.desc: Nursing manager Sarah hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, taking inspiration from her husband's Middle Eastern heritage to offer her guests a Turkish fusion menu. A hilarious evening lies in store, with unexpected tattoos, beauty tips and twerking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Would-be clairvoyant Janet hosts the second of a week of dinner parties in Leeds and hopes her ghoul-themed dinner party will help her win the cash prize. With more surprises in store, will the fright-night theme be enough to ghost Janet into the lead?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Record label owner Phil hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, hoping to win the £1,000 prize money by serving up a 1970s rock-themed menu. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

The wander down memory lane features a stuffed whole trout, potted shrimp and a retro crab cocktail, plus bread from Mary's favourite bakery. Plus, Jimmy rustles up a gorgeously gooey mozzarella and pesto bread. Mum's best friend comes round to try on a new dress, and Jim is distraught after his dog disappears. Comedy, guest starring Tracy-Ann Oberman, with Simon Bird and Tom Rosenthal

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam tries to celebrate his birthday, but the evening is spoiled when Sheila Bloom, who is famously obsessed with her car, makes catty remarks about his vehicle. His father then reveals he used to date her years ago, upsetting Mum in the process. Finally, the family abandons dinner and heads to a Chinese restaurant, only for Sheila to reappear, forcing Dad to explain what the pair really got up to

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: Adam is shocked to find his mother has set him up on a date by inviting Tanya Green to dinner, and his father has dressed in a suit for the occasion. Meanwhile, Jim is locked out of his house when his dog swallows his keys. Comedy, starring Simon Bird

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: The youngest of the conservancy's eight ostriches prepares for mating, and the rangers hope this will lead to increased numbers for their dwindling flock of the birds. A lioness searches for her three cubs, which have gone missing following a buffalo attack, and a one-armed baboon attempts to ingratiate himself with a new troop after being rejected by his own

Horror adapted from Sarah Waters's novel, starring Domhnall Gleeson, Will Poulter and Ruth Wilson

Craig Charles tries to get a smart lighting system playing properly with his television in a bid to experience the ultimate immersive home cinema. Ortis Deley and Georgie Barrat face-off in this week's challenge, pitting high-end against entry-level cooking tech, with the results judged by a revered food critic. Plus, a piece of tech from the archives - the humble 35mm camera -.is reappraised in the modern age, and Jordan Erica Webber previews the big gaming releases of 2021

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Susan and her campervan Helen arrive on home turf, touring the breath-taking southern Highlands of Scotland on a 140-mile trek from Glasgow to Aberfoyle. At Loch Lomond, the comedian takes to a kayak and hits the water to get a unique view of the amazing local scenery. She visits the spectacular Falls of Dochart, where she gets special access to the stunningly beautiful island of Inchbuie. She also views a bizarre Iron Age-style dwelling, known as a crannog, erected on stilts in Loch Tay

programme.title: Big Weekends with Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: Gregg heads to Barcelona, uncovering the city's artistic side in the incredible architecture of Antoni Gaudi, most famous for the Sagrada Familia, a large unfinished Roman Catholic minor basilica. The MasterChef presenter learns about the city's other artistic sons, while doing a self-portrait in the style of Pablo Picasso. He also visits the street festival in the Gracia neighbourhood, which culminates in the breathtaking castellers, or 'human towers', and samples the array of back-street tapas restaurants

programme.title: Greatest Hits of the

Contributors include Midge Ure, Shirlie Kemp, Steve Levine, Paul Morley, Malcolm Gerrie, David Grant and Katie Puckrik

programme.title: George Michael, the Highs, the Lows and All the Hits
programme.desc: Documentary examining the troubled last years of the pop star, culminating in his death on Christmas Day in 2016. The programme examines the contrast between his inner demons and his beloved public persona during four decades of chart success, which saw him rise to fame alongside childhood friend Andrew Ridgeley in Wham!, before embarking on a solo career that secured his place as one of the leading singer-songwriters in the world

programme.desc: The actress visits a mansion in the Surrey Hills, where her host is self-made millionaire Pete, who was the youngest of seven children and hails from Lancashire. Sally noses around his 13 bathrooms, as well as the £200,000 cinema room, and gets a taste of the high life - from a helicopter ride to a local vineyard, to a big night in with a private chef. The luxury trip is capped off with a jaunt on the water in a million-pound speedboat.

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Palaces
programme.desc: A look at the history of Holyrood House in Edinburgh, which is the official royal residence in Scotland and saw bloody struggles for power when it was home to Mary, Queen of Scots. Kate Williams tells the story of a royal tomb raider and his ill-gotten booty from the grave of Charles I

programme.title: Princess Margaret: Queen of Mustique
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the princess's visits to the Caribbean island, the place where she felt most at home and most able to be herself. From scandalous affairs, X-rated party games, a rumoured rock star lover and even an alleged bank robbery, Mustique was where Princess Margaret could behave outrageously, away from prying eyes, at least most of the time. Archive footage, insider testimony and expert interviews combine to find out what the original royal party girl really got up to on her Caribbean hideaway

programme.title: Jane McDonald & Friends
programme.desc: The host sings some of her favourite songs with a band, inviting stars of the stage, screen and music world including actor John Partridge, Rose Royce frontwoman Gwen Dickey, and the cast of the record-breaking West End musical Chicago to join her. programme.title: 49th Parallel (1941)

Comedy Western, starring John Wayne, Maureen O'Hara, Yvonne de Carlo, Patrick Wayne and Stefanie Powers

programme.title: The Pink Panther (1963)
programme.desc: The world's most inept police detective, Inspector Clouseau, heads off to Switzerland in search of a suave jewel thief who has purloined a priceless diamond from a princess - but the bumbling officer's every ruse to foil the crime ends in chaos, not least when he is framed for the robbery himself. Blake Edwards' comedy, starring Peter Sellers, David Niven, Capucine, Robert Wagner and Claudia Cardinale

programme.title: City Slickers (1991)
programme.desc: Three middle-aged men take a break from the rat race and their mid-life crises to join a two-week cattle drive from New Mexico to Colorado. Together with a bunch of fellow urbanites, they find the going much tougher than expected, and it is down to a real-life cowboy to whip them into shape. Comedy adventure, starring Billy Crystal, Daniel Stern, Bruno Kirby and Jack Palance, who won an Oscar for his performance as grizzled trail boss Curly

programme.title: The Martian Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Ridley Scott and stars Kate Mara and Chiwetel Ejiofor talk about making the 2015 sci-fi adventure, which stars Matt Damon as an astronaut stranded on Mars

programme.title: The Martian (2015)
programme.desc: A manned mission to Mars is abruptly abandoned, with one crew member left for dead. However, the astronaut has survived and faces a series of challenges - to contact Earth and arrange his rescue and to survive for the years it will take for a spacecraft to reach him on resources intended to last the crew a month. Ridley Scott's sci-fi drama based on the novel by Andy Weir, starring Matt Damon, Jessica Chastain, Kristen Wiig and Chiwetel Ejiofor

programme.title: Ex Machina (2015)
programme.desc: A computer programmer working for the world's biggest search engine is given the opportunity to spend a week at the remote home of his boss, a hi-tech genius. The break turns out to be an elaborate experiment, in which he must test the world's first truly artificial intelligence, which is housed in the body of a beautiful robot girl. Sci-fi drama, starring Domhnall Gleeson, Alicia Vikander and Oscar Isaac

programme.title: I, Frankenstein (2014)
programme.desc: The infamous scientist's monster roams the Earth 200 years after burying his creator, and is drawn into an ancient supernatural conflict. Two factions of magical beings are at war, and the key to ending the conflict lies in tapping the secret of the creature's immortality. Fantasy adventure, starring Aaron Eckhart, Bill Nighy and Miranda Otto

programme.title: A Shot in the Dark (1964)
programme.desc: The bungling Inspector Clouseau is sent to investigate a high-profile murder case. All the clues point to a maid being the guilty party, but the accident-prone detective is instantly smitten with her, and devotes his efforts to proving her innocence - despite all evidence to the contrary. Comedy, starring Peter Sellers, Elke Sommer, George Sanders and Herbert Lom

programme.title: Ice Age (2002)
programme.desc: Three mismatched prehistoric animals join forces to return a human infant to its tribe - but as their journey unfolds, the creatures fail to anticipate the many perils and pitfalls that lay in store for them. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of John Leguizamo, Ray Romano, Denis Leary, Goran Visnjic and Jack Black

programme.title: Madagascar (2005)
programme.desc: A gang of streetwise penguins decides to escape from New York's Central Park Zoo and return to a life in the wild, but when Marty the zebra and his friends follow suit, they end up stranded in Africa on a jungle island without a clue about how to fend for themselves. Animated comedy, with the voices of Ben Stiller, David Schwimmer, Chris Rock, Jada Pinkett Smith and Sacha Baron Cohen

programme.title: Lemony Snicket's A Series of Unfortunate Events (2004)
programme.desc: Three orphans are entrusted into the care of their sinister uncle, a master of disguise who intends to kill them for the family fortune. Quickly realising he is up to no good, the children flee, moving from one prospective guardian to the next - but their wily relative is never far behind. Comedy adventure based on the children's books, starring Jim Carrey, Meryl Streep, Jude Law, Liam Aitken and Emily Browning, with Kara and Shelby Hoffman

programme.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past (2014)
programme.desc: In a future ravaged by war, the superhero team makes a desperate last stand against a force of deadly robots. Their only hope is to send Wolverine back in time to the 1970s, to join forces with an earlier version of the X-Men and change the events that started the conflict. Superhero adventure sequel uniting the casts of the original movie and prequel First Class, starring Hugh Jackman, Jennifer Lawrence, James McAvoy and Michael Fassbender

programme.title: Hitman: Agent 47 (2015)
programme.desc: The latest in a long line of genetically engineered clones bred to be the perfect assassin takes on a mission that strikes close to home. A corporation intends to use the technology that created him to build an army of superhuman killers. As the assassin fights to bring them down, he uncovers the dark secrets surrounding his origin. Action thriller, starring Rupert Friend, Hannah Ware and Zachary Quinto

programme.title: World War Z (2013)
programme.desc: A virus sweeps across the globe, turning everyone infected into mindless, ravening zombies. As entire countries fall into chaos, a United Nations investigator sets off on a journey around the world to discover the cause of the pandemic and hopefully find a cure before human civilisation is brought to an end. Thriller based on the novel by Max Brooks, starring Brad Pitt and Mireille Enos

programme.title: Horns (2013)
programme.desc: A man who has become an outcast after being accused of murdering his own girlfriend wakes up one morning to find he has grown horns. He discovers he now has the power to compel people to confess their sins or act on their darkest desires, and uses his supernatural gifts to expose the real killer. Horror based on Joe Hill's novel, starring Daniel Radcliffe and Juno Temple

programme.title: The Perfect Catch (2005)
programme.desc: Unlikely couple Ben and Lindsey meet and fall in love over the winter. When summer brings a new baseball season, Lindsey discovers Ben's devotion to the Boston Red Sox baseball team knows no bounds - and suspects she will always come second. The Farrelly brothers' romantic comedy, starring Jimmy Fallon and Drew Barrymore, inspired by Nick Hornby's football-themed novel Fever Pitch, but with America's favourite sport replacing Britain's

Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs

programme.title: Gentlemen Prefer Blondes (1953)
programme.desc: Lounge singers Lorelei and Dorothy embark on a transatlantic cruise to Paris in search of rich husbands. While Dorothy falls for a penniless man, Lorelei thinks she has found the millionaire of her dreams, only for his father to hire a private eye to spy on her and prove she is a gold-digger. Howard Hawks' musical comedy, starring Marilyn Monroe, Jane Russell, Charles Coburn and Elliott Reid

Period drama from James Ivory, based on EM Forster's novel and starring Helena Bonham Carter, Maggie Smith, Denholm Elliott, Daniel Day-Lewis, Julian Sands and Judi Dench

Raouf, Chrissie, Clarkey and Katie dig deep to find fault but can only find small problems. Following an afternoon in the billiard room, the guests gush over their friendship at dinner but, when it comes to the question of future meet-ups, Vicky drops a bombshell

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Sports enthusiast David treats his guests to a traditional darts competition, adjudicated by former world champion Alan Warriner-Little, but he is unimpressed when Paula sends back her evening meal. However, it is Chris and Jacqui's feedback that disappoints the hosts the most

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Bateman's Mill near Chesterfield, where manager Jade Hendon has high hopes of being the best. But she faces an uphill battle when David and Susan go out of their way to find hairs in the bathroom - and to make mattters worse, her Derbyshire breakfast is not a hit with Chris and Jacqui

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hoteliers get together one last time to confront their rivals, defend any criticism and find out what they have been paid. However, things get particularly heated when it's time to count up the cash

programme.title: Darcey Bussell's Wild Coasts of Scotland
programme.desc: The former ballerina and ex-Strictly Come Dancing judge explores the islands off the west coast of Scotland and finds out more about her Scottish ancestry. Her odyssey begins on the Isle of Skye, where she tries scallops fresh from a loch, has a bash at step dancing and goes wild swimming in honour of her grandfather

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary catching up with London's Crossrail engineers less than two years after the project's excavations uncovered the 14th-century Charterhouse plague pit. In an extraordinary turn of events, further work on the Liverpool Street station has revealed another burial ground, this time accommodating the capital's radicals, rebels and outsiders. The Bedlam burial ground, which is thought to be the final resting place of nearly 20,000 people, was believed to be lost for around 300 years. Now, with the excavation process under way, archaeologists and historians have been using bone analysis techniques in conjunction with parish records to reveal the names and life stories of those whose remains were found on-site. Their research reveals that people came from as far afield as Africa to work in the English capital, and sheds light on how pollution first became one of the city's most prolific killers

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Medics at King's College Hospital treat 79-year-old Arthur, who has been brought into resus with acute stomach discomfort and has several underlying conditions that could be to blame. Meanwhile, 20-year-old wine merchant Tom is rushed into A&E after collapsing with sharp chest pains and breathing difficulties, and retired soldier Rockey is concerned about his blurred vision

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition sees staff at King's College Hospital in south London treat patients involved in serious road accidents. Former soldier Steve, 48, was found unconscious at the wheel, 11-year-old schoolgirl Scarlett has been knocked down by a car, and Pauline, 70, was hit by a vehicle that mounted the pavement, seriously injuring her lower legs

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock is joined by Andrew Flintoff to battle guest captain Bill Bailey and team-mate Roisin Conaty as Jimmy Carr hosts another edition of the game show mash-up. Susie Dent is ably assisted by Rob Beckett in the Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley deals with the numbers and letters

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star
